fields.description:
# plan-review fan-out optimization: token + wall-clock (caching, merge, batch, parallel) — fidelity-preserving

## Reproduction Steps
1. `rebar review-plan <epic>` on an epic with N children (observed N=8).
2. Review runs >25 min with no completion (appears hung).

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: epic review completes in minutes at reasonable token cost.
- Actual: container criteria G3/G4 run as `2*N` SEQUENTIAL agentic LLM calls (~75-150s each;
  N=8 ⇒ 16 calls ≈ 27 min). Each call re-sends the WHOLE parent plan + sibling roster; G3/G4 send
  the same (parent,child) context twice — large token waste atop the latency.

## Root cause + provenance (PROVEN)
- `pass1.py:_run_container` runs `for crit in [G3,G4]: for child in children: pass1_container(...)`
  SEQUENTIALLY, OUTSIDE the ThreadPoolExecutor that parallelizes every other tier (agentic, request
  budget ≈13). Proven via faulthandler stacks (blocked on network, not spinning) + per-pairing logs
  (pairings complete and advance) ⇒ bounded-but-slow, not a loop.
- NOT a workflow-migration regression. Authored in the BESPOKE harness at commit 832b3af0 (story ca03 /
  dry-frost-zenith, "ca03 AC5", 2026-06-23) for CONTEXT-WINDOW FITTING while keeping content whole
  ("NO TRUNCATION, NEVER CHUNK CONTENT … the per-child pairing is the lever to fit context — never the
  content"). Migration extracted it verbatim. NOT a partial-re-review mechanism (container loop has zero
  checkpoint/scoping; the delta-re-review feature boil-golem-veto/grace-thud-feint is separate and uses a
  tripwire probe, having rejected subset-reuse).
- Insight: G3 (child-coverage) and G4 (child-consistency) are inherently CROSS-CHILD criteria; the
  per-child slice was a window concession that WEAKENS cross-child analysis. Batching whole children
  where the window allows can IMPROVE fidelity, not just cut cost.

## Workflow-engine impact (scoped in)
Most of this is INSIDE the ProductionBatchRunner (`run_pass1`/`pass1.py`/`passes.py`/`sizing.py`), which the
engine invokes as ONE opaque `batch` step (`interpreter.py:371-396` copies the result dict wholesale, never
inspects internals). The interpreter/executor/YAML/step-kinds need NO change for parallelize/merge/batch.
The shared seam is the runner (`RunRequest` + `runner.py`), used by BOTH `passes.py` (Pass-1) and the engine
`RunnerAgentStep` (`workflow/runs.py:207` — verify/decide/coach/grounding + other workflows). Caching is
applied at that shared seam AND extended ENGINE-WIDE to RunnerAgentStep (pulled into scope per request).

## Plan (composable; all preserve ca03's "content always whole" guarantee)

### 1. Prompt caching (CONFIRMED feasible + provider-safe; matches OSS precedent: Arize/phoenix, code-graph-rag, shotgun)
Three pydantic-ai 1.107.0 mechanisms compose (≤3 of the 4 allowed breakpoints):
- **`CachePoint`** in the USER-message content list: `[stable_prefix, CachePoint(ttl="5m"), volatile_suffix]`
  — caches tools+system+parent-plan on Anthropic; SILENTLY filtered on openai/gemini/mistral (no error,
  no gating). Used for the whole-parent-plan prefix re-sent across all bins.
- **`anthropic_cache_instructions=True`** — caches the static system prompt across calls (engine-wide:
  RunnerAgentStep + all workflows).
- **`anthropic_cache_tool_definitions=True`** — caches the tool surface.
- The two `anthropic_cache_*` keys are Anthropic-ONLY → GATE on the resolved provider (`runner.py:201`,
  `resolved.startswith("anthropic")`); `CachePoint` is the portable one.
Fidelity-NEUTRAL (identical inputs/outputs). Scope: the shared `RunRequest`/`runner.py` change + populate
the prefix in `passes.py` (Pass-1/container) AND in the engine `RunnerAgentStep` (`workflow/runs.py`) so
verify/decide/coach + completion-verification + code-review all cache their stable prefix.

### 2. Warm-then-fan-out (the caching⨯parallelization interaction — CRITICAL)
Anthropic caching is write-once/read-many: the cache is only readable AFTER the first response BEGINS.
Firing all bins concurrently ⇒ ALL miss + ALL write (≈16×1.25 ≈ 20× input cost, zero read benefit).
Therefore parallelization MUST be: run ONE warming call first, await its first streamed token, THEN fan out
the rest concurrently ⇒ 1 write + N-1 reads (≈2.75× vs 20×). Optional: pre-warm at gate start with a
throwaway call. This couples #1 and #3 — they are co-designed, not independent.

### 3. Parallelize + fix the budget cap
Submit the (warmed) merged+batched bins into the existing ThreadPoolExecutor (replace the sequential loop).
Fix the per-plan cap that scales UP with child count (`sizing.py` centrality `base*(1+centrality)`) so
shedding engages for large epics instead of being disabled by the very signal that should trigger it.

### 4. Merge G3 + G4 into one call per bin (2N → N)
Same context, disjoint audits the container prompt already describes together. Keep per-criterion
attribution in output. Behind an offline eval.

### 5. Child bin-packing (analogous to the facet criteria batcher)
Pack multiple SMALL whole children into one call up to a token budget = model-window × P8_HEADROOM − reserve
(reuse the `pair_tokens`/`budget` + P8 machinery). PRESERVE the single-child path when parent+child strains
the window; a child that alone exceeds budget stays the "too-big → reduce the ticket" finding. Each child
WHOLE. Mitigate per-child attention with structured per-child sections + REQUIRED per-child output. Bonus:
improves G4 cross-child consistency. Behind an offline eval.

## Caching gotchas → design constraints (from OSS research; cite in tests)
- TTL: each cache READ refreshes the 5m window free; a WRITE starts it. With warm-then-parallelize the
  fan-out finishes inside one 5m window → keep `ttl="5m"` (cheaper writes). Use `"1h"` only for genuinely
  sequential stretches with >5-min gaps.
- Byte-stability: NO timestamps/UUIDs/run-ids/unsorted-JSON/variable tool-order in the cached prefix; keep
  dynamic content AFTER the CachePoint or in a separate (non-cached) message part. Dynamic system-prompt
  functions invalidate the instructions cache.
- Breakpoint budget: instructions + tool_definitions + CachePoint = 3/4 (safe). Do NOT also enable
  `anthropic_cache_messages` without confirming ≤4 and the oldest-first eviction is acceptable.
- Min prefix: 4096 tokens on Opus 4.8 (parent plan clears it; a small Haiku judge step may silently NOT
  cache — verify, don't assume).
- 20-block lookback: a single agentic call that appends >20 tool blocks can miss its own cache; add an
  intermediate CachePoint ~every 15 blocks if a container call runs a long tool loop.
- OpenRouter-routed Anthropic/Gemini ALSO drops CachePoint (pydantic-ai #3604) — don't assume it caches.
- Watch pydantic-ai open issues: #3453 (4-breakpoint/history reuse), #3722 (no cache on assistant turns).
- Verify structured-output (PromptedOutput) interaction with a targeted test — some projects strip
  anthropic_cache_* during tool-output/thinking phases.

## Sequencing (risk-ordered)
- (1)+(2) caching at the shared runner seam + warm-then-fan-out — fidelity-neutral, biggest win.
- (3) parallelize + budget-cap fix — wall-clock.
- Engine-wide: extend caching to RunnerAgentStep (verify/decide/coach + other workflows).
- (4) merge G3+G4 and (5) bin-packing — each behind an offline eval, single-child fallback preserved.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Caching at the shared runner seam: `CachePoint` inserted into user content (stable prefix first);
      `anthropic_cache_instructions`/`anthropic_cache_tool_definitions` enabled and GATED to anthropic
      providers; on openai/gemini the call still succeeds unchanged (provider-safe).
- [ ] Engine-wide: RunnerAgentStep (`workflow/runs.py`) caches its stable system-prompt/tool prefix, so
      verify/decide/coach and other workflows (completion-verification, code-review) benefit — not just plan-review.
- [ ] Warm-then-fan-out: the container fan-out warms the cache with one call before parallelizing the rest;
      on Anthropic `usage.cache_read_input_tokens` is non-zero on the fanned-out calls (NOT all writes).
- [ ] Byte-stability: the cached prefix carries no per-call interpolation (asserted by a stability test);
      cache reads stay non-zero across the fan-out.
- [ ] Parallel: bins run concurrently in the pool; the budget cap no longer disables shedding as N grows.
- [ ] Merge: G3+G4 in one call per bin with correct per-criterion attribution; offline eval shows no regression.
- [ ] Bin-packing: small children packed to the window budget; parent+child-too-large keeps its own call;
      each child whole; required per-child output; offline eval shows no per-child attention loss.
- [ ] Instrumentation: cache_read/write tokens per run are surfaced (observability); an alert fires if reads
      stay 0 across a fan-out (broken warm-up / silent invalidator detector).
- [ ] Measured: 8-child epic completes in minutes with materially lower token cost; findings within eval
      tolerance of current behavior. `make lint` + full suite pass.
